Now the problem that a lot of us as experts have because we're so smart and we know our material and our ideas so well, then we think, okay guys, this is what we're going to create for you and we just start giving people what we think that they want. And sometimes you get lucky and you have success because of that. But the majority of times it's actually, it's not, the majority of times you're going to screw up what they actually want. We know they want this result, but we have to make sure we customize this in a way that they want. If you get better at the customization, the better the results are going to be and the more likely and more happy they're going to be with that. Okay, so if I'm giving this new vehicle, I'm like, what do you guys want? Like, I want a CD player. Like, sweet, I'm putting a CD player in that new vehicle, whatever it might be. Okay, so that's kind of the analogy now to put it into practical terms. When I first got started in this business, some of my first coaches were Frank Kern and Ed Dale. They had a course called Underachievers. You guys remember that? Which was awesome for those of you who remember it. I ended up actually buying the brand from them a couple of years later because it was so cool. We haven't talked about it. We shut down that whole company probably seven or eight years ago, but I love the concept. One of the core things they taught in that was this, is that there's a three-step process to be successful online. It's so simple, yet it's like the backwards. Most of us, we come in and we're like, okay, I have this idea for this product. You create the product and then you're like, oh, it's done. You try to shove down people's throats and you try to get them to buy the thing. That's sometimes you get lucky, but most of the time that's the issue. If you look at the system, it's step number one is find a hot market. That's what we did earlier today, making sure you're finding here's the three markets. Here's the sub market. Here's our niche within there, but we know that's a hot market based on what we figured out and talked about earlier today. The second step then is not go create a product and sell it to them. Second step is ask them what they actually want. What do you guys actually want? You want to do that? Cool. Then third step is then give that to them. Let's not find the market, create a product and shove it down their throats. Let's find a market, ask them what they want, and then just give it to them. It's pretty simple. Guess how we came up with click funnels? Every now and then our market is like, I wish we had an easy way to build funnels. All right. Hey Todd, can you build that? Yeah man. Sweet, let's build that and give it to them. That was it, right? It's pretty much, I mean there's little details that he had to do. I don't know what it was, but it seemed easy. We just had to ask them what they want. This is the key is this middle step here. Ryan the Vech, we're a really good book called Ask. I'm sure some of you guys have read it. I like the principles, but for most of us, it's way intense. We had a higher team of people to do it for us and it was awesome. That's what I'm talking about here. This is a very simplified version of that. Something that all of us can implement. In fact, all of you all are going to implement that tonight. We're going to build one. Everyone's going to do it. It's going to be really, really fun. That's what it is. What an Ask campaign is, is basically when we first started doing them, they were basically landing page look like this. A lot of you guys already have stuff happening. Those who don't yet, in the book we talked about, a lot of people reading the book aren't going to have any kind of business up front. What I recommend in the book is for everyone is initially we need to get results for your customers first. Putting on a free class or something to get results for people first. The concept is I'm going to put on a class. In fact, one of my friends did this when he was trying to get clients. He built a class. It was called Ethical Cult Building. It was a free class. Everyone can come in. He taught the course. Then from there, he got tons of clients. He could have then gone, stuff we'll talk about tomorrow and built into a whole company, but he didn't. The concept was he had this class out there and he put it out there. He said it was free. It was basically asking me a question about whatever question you have. We're going to take those questions and from there we'll build the curriculum. It was an amazing experience. I had a great time going through his course. This Ask campaign, a couple of stories just to show you guys the power in it. One of my businesses, as you may know, we have a company called Overcomponography. We first came up with the idea for the company. We started putting it all together. I was assuming that all of our customers would be men who are addicted to pornography. The product started getting created towards them and all the marketing and everything. About that time, I heard about these Ask campaigns. I was like, okay, I'm going to do that. We put it out on the page. It was really simple. All I said is what's your number one question about how to overcome pornography addiction? We put it out there. We started buying, this is pre-Facebooks. We were buying Google ads to it. We were going to different parenting groups. What was interesting, and this completely transformed the direction of the entire company, is that the people that started filling this form out were not the people who I thought I thought they were going to be men who were struggling with pornography addiction. It was wives of men who were struggling. It was mothers that had kids that were struggling. Those were the people that responded. All the questions came in like, well, my husband is struggling. What should I do? My kids having this issue. All these questions started coming in. I was like, wow, the people that are actually interested in this, it's a whole different mark. I missed the mark completely. I was so close to it. It transformed everything from how we built the products, how we built this. Everything changed because we figured that out. That's the power in these things. It helps you figure out exactly what your people want, and then you just give them what they ask for. It's the easiest way to make money. It's the most simple thing in the world. You don't have to reinvent the wheel. It's just asking what they want. Let's see. I want to make sure we got this. Basically what we do is we're going to ask these people, what do you want in this vehicle? What are the things? John's going to come up here in a few minutes and he's going to be showing a couple different ways to do this. It used to be we'd create a page like this and we'd drive Google ads to it or Facebook ads or whatever it is. This method still works. A lot of times nowadays there's easier, covert ways to do it that are especially in Facebook to make it really, really easy. John's going to show you guys some of those things. My goal though is to get people to come through this ask campaign and I'm going to ask them that one question and it's simple. You do have to over complicate it. In fact, I think what page in the book do we have of this script? Page 38? All right, cool, I found it. It's on this page. These are not numbered for some reason, so that's my bad. It's a page that has the same image on it right here. On the side here is a script. What I would do if I had a landing page, this would be the video I'd put on the page. If I'm doing a Facebook live, this is the script I would use. If I was doing an ad, there's a bunch of different ways to do it, but this is basically the script. It says, there we go, next page. It's on this page. There's no page numbers in here. Chapter ask campaign. It's like a quarter in the way. Yeah, just a quarter. All right. Here it is. All right. Just listen. I'll say it. It'll be good. Ken, I'll say it and then it's on this page when you guys do find it. Basically, this is the script. Dear friend, my name is Russell Brunson. I know your time is valuable, so I'm going to get right to the point. I need your advice. I need your advice. I'm putting the finishing touches on a new course called How to Make Money in Your Underwear, How to Biohack, How to Lose Weight, Whatever Your Thing is, whatever your unique opportunity is. I want to make sure I don't leave anything out. If you let me know, your biggest question is about how to overcome weight loss, overcome whatever, overcome doggy addiction, how to make a potato gun, whatever your thing is. It could be anything. You may even think it's silly. All you have to do is type in your question in the box below and click submit. In exchange for your advice, I'll give you free access to my new course that's happening on June 16th. This course can sell for $197 in the future, but you get special access to free to come. All you have to do is let me know your number one question. A couple of reasons why we're doing this. The first reason, I'll go back and put a slide into this because I think it's important. If you want to take somebody to here and you want to give them the result, one of the biggest problems a lot of people have is they come in and like, I'm going to be an expert. I'm going to take someone up here. I know how to do it. They're like, I'm the greatest person in the world. You should come listen to me. I'll take you on this journey. There's no proof that they actually know what they're talking about. I got two stories to tell about this. The first one was, I have a friend. He was a high schooler at the time. His name is Alec Jetzel. Alec wanted to get a job. Probably five or six years ago. During summer, he applied at a whole bunch of different places. Everyone basically turned him down. He didn't get a job. He was like, I'm a talented kid. I work hard. They should give me a job. Eventually, he went down to the mall and he found his favorite clothing store. He's like, I'm going to get a job there. He walked in and he was going to apply and all that stuff. He's just like, man, they're not going to hire me. He walked up to one of the managers. He's like, hey, I know this store is kind of messed up, closed everywhere. Do you mind? I'm just kind of like OCD a little bit. Do you mind if I just clean things up? The manager's like, if you want, whatever. It's a high school kid sitting in a mall store. He's like, yeah, if you want to do my work for me, that's awesome. Alec goes in and cleans up the things, folds the clothes. All nice and everything. He comes back and he's like, hey, thanks. Appreciate you let me work. I'll see you later. It took off. I'm sure the people that worked there were just like, that's weird. Why did that guy just do all my job for me? Next day, Alec came back and he came to the store again. He's like, hey guys, how are you doing? Good? He's like, hey, do you mind if I help? Clean up again? Yeah, if you want. He's got his folding clothes. He's doing his whole thing. The manager, one of the manager owners, someone came out and was like, what are you doing? He's like, I just love this place that I'd help clean up. He's like, you want a job? He's like, yes, I do. And boom, you got a job that fast. And it came from this. I'm like giving results first. If you look at my timeline, we did coaching for business opportunity. People like five or, I don't know, longer than that. Seven or eight years ago. And then we stopped. And for about three year period of time, I went back into the business. That's when we started launching our supplement funnels, our dating funnel, like all these different businesses because I wanted to get back into the business and we were doing it for a long time. And afterwards, after two or three years, I was like, I want to get back into this business. We kind of want to do the coaching stuff again. And so the first thing I didn't do was make a video and be like, hey, my name's Russell Brunson. I'm so cool. I got tons of results. I'm the most cool guy in the world. You should pay me to be in my coaching program. But how many of you guys have seen people like that? That's their pitch for their coaching programs. Super annoying. I can't stand that. And I was like, I got to make sure that my stuff actually works. I want to take someone's path to get a result and then they fail. And then I'm like, man, I'm actually not any good at this. And so I started thinking back to the whole .com secrets book. Like who is my dream client? Who do I want to serve? And the time I was going through a juicing thing and I saw Drew Canole online. I was following him. I was one of his 1.5 million followers and we're hanging out and I'm like learning all the stuff about all this juicing stuff. And I was having so much fun. And then he stopped selling me things. And I was like, dang, I wish this guy would sell me something else. And then I was like, he would be my dream client. I would love to come in here because there's so much low hanging fruit he doesn't even know about. And I was like, I'm going to be cool to come in and actually help them. And then a week or two later I was flying to San Diego and we had a friend in common. So that's my friend. He introduced me to Drew. I'd love to get to know him. So he introduced me to Drew through text messages like, hey, Drew, this guy's Russell Brunson is going to be in San Diego. You guys should meet up. And Drew's like, cool, here's my address, which is kind of weird anyway. But I'm like, all right. So I was like, I'll come to this house. So I jumped in my very first Uber ever. It was my first Uber ride. I was really excited. And I go up to this place and I get to this house and knock on the door and he opens the door. And I was like, this is like, I was freaking out. Like, you know, I was like the star struck like this is Drew Canole. I'm so excited. And then he lets me in this house and sit down and I walk into the, to like the front room. I sit down and I see my book, the 108 split tests sitting on the counter. I was like, oh man, like, I didn't know if he knew who I was. I was like, you bought my book. Like, yes. Like, it's so cool. Like, it's so much easier to negotiate with people when you call them up and they're like, I'm a huge fan. You're like, oh yes, this is going to be so much easier. Anyway, so I get there. I was like, hey, you got my book. He's like, yeah. And he kind of gives me this weird look. He's like, there's a really funny story about that. You want to hear it? I'm like, yeah. He's like, last week I started seeing your face pop on my newsfeed all the time. So eventually I like clicked on it. I see this book. So I buy it because it's free. It shows at my house. He said, last night I'm sitting there flipping through the book and I was like, man, how cool would it be to have Russell come and like fix our funnels. And then I get a text from Carl like five minutes later and now you're sitting in my front room. And he's like, this is kind of creepy. And I was like, that is really creepy, man. And so we start talking and like just kind of hanging out. And from talking with him, first off, all of us, right, we try to be very professional and not vulnerable and we're talking and we're talking about business and stuff. And then he kind of got a little bit vulnerable for a second. And I was like, man, I feel like something was wrong. It wasn't like there was something. They were struggling with someone in their business. I don't know what it was, but I was just like, is everything going OK? He's like, yeah, things are good. He's like, we're having some struggles here. It's kind of hard to be profitable sometimes and all this kind of stuff. But we're making over a million bucks a year, the posturing. But I can tell something was there. And I was like, well, if you want, I got to go back home with my kids. But I could fly out here for a day and just work with your team and kind of look at stuff and see if I could help at all if you'd be interested. And he was like, I can't really afford that right now. And I was like, well, let's do it for free. He's like, well, why would you do it for free? I'm like, I don't know. You're Drew Canole. This would be so fun. If you promise take me somewhere we can do something together, I'm in. It would be so much fun. And finally, after a bunch of convincing, he's like, fine. I don't know where your angle is, but if you want to come out, whatever. So a month later, I fly out. I come to his office. And in fact, if you watch the Funnel Hacker video, where I'm sketching on the white on the windows, that was his office. They didn't have whiteboards or anything. You were just in this random place. And so I'm trying to show him funnels on the mirror, on the windows. And when I first get there, though, I ask them, I'm like, I can't help you get to here unless I know where you're really at. And so can you show me what's happening? And so they had everyone on their team. They had the count and was there and everyone. And the count was like, you want to see our numbers? And I was like, yeah. He looks at Drew. Can we show him his numbers? Drew's like, yeah, let's show him our numbers. So he shows me the numbers. And I was just like, oh my gosh. I didn't realize it was, at the time, they were losing about $50,000 a month. And I was like, how long has this been happening for? And it was almost a year. I'm like, how much longer until you're done? And he's like, we're pretty close. I'm like, oh my gosh. Crap. OK, this went from a fun little side project to oh my gosh, this is a real thing. And so when in there, we started looking at their current funnels and there were some things that they kind of weren't doing or whatever. We talked through those. And then I was talking about what are the new opportunities? Like, I was a customer. And I bought all your stuff. And then there was nothing else to buy. What else do you guys have? And we're thinking about this and this and this. And we had this idea for a supplement to be a green drink. I was like, dude, that's it. And I'm like, I do supplements. I'm going to show you my supplement funnels. I showed them my supplement funnel. I showed them the company that named my supplements. So they called them up. And eventually they came back. They got the domain name Organifi. And then they named it. And they put the other thing in. They put every last penny they had into this supplement. They were like, when it launched, it was like, if this doesn't go, like we're done, like the whole company shut down. And we've boxed back and forth next month or so. We helped them kind of structure out the strategy behind it and the pricing and everything. And then they went and they launched it. And day one, it was like $20,000 in sales. Day two was $30,000. It kept going up from there. And in the last about two and a half years, they launched, they'd done over $25 million with that funnel, which is crazy, right? And so what was cool was afterwards, then Drew made a video of him. And it helps that he's probably the best looking human on earth. But he made this video just telling the story about how he helped him. I took that video, I put it on a web page, and said, I'm looking for my dream clients. They watched Drew's video on those button below so it said apply if you want to work with me. Like it wasn't even my face on anywhere. And that's where we launched the inner circle. And it went from one member to 10 to 10 to 15, 15 to 20. At that point, we started having someone like Liz Benny. Liz came in and we gave her a huge transformation. And Liz came, we flew her to Boise and we filmed her story. And that became another video. We kept adding and kept laying around. And it went from 20 people to 40, 40 to 60, 60 to 80, 80 to 100. And I was like, let's stop. And we got 100 people in our circle. But it wasn't about me pounding my own horn. Like, look how great I am. I got someone a result, my dream client, my person I want to work with. And I put that out there. And what happened is that people like Drew started applying. How many fitness people do we have in this room right now? It's insane. Drew's people started applying. Liz's people started applying. Our event used to be predominantly men. Now we've got half of your women, which is so exciting. That's because of Liz's video. And those things we're putting out there are attracting in our dream clients. But the result is key first. Like, getting the result first, and then we can share that. And so the whole concept of doing the Ask Campaign this way is like, let's do a live at the studio, a workshop, let's do a weekend thing, or a six hour thing, or three, or something that you're going to get some results for people. And it's bringing in a beta group of people. And let's take them through this process so you get results. So some of you guys already have results. Those who don't, this is where I would start at. I said, next Saturday we're doing a three hour class. I'm going to show you guys how to go from this to this. And we're going to go through this process together and make it free. And you're like, I don't want to do it for free, because then we're going to buy in the future. Doesn't matter. Right now you got to do it for free to get the right people in. So we run this Ask Campaign and say, look, next Saturday I'm going to do a free workshop. I'm going to show you guys how to blank without blank, whatever it might be, what's your number of questions about this thing, fill out the survey, and you can come to my workshop for free next Saturday. And get a whole bunch of people in. Get 50 people. Get 100 people to come in and go through this process with you. Whatever it is you teach. And as you go through that process with them, then you're going to come out the other side, and you're going to have some amazing success stories. And those success stories become the foundation for building the rest of your business. Any business I go into right now, the first thing I'm doing is like, hey, this is a cool product. Let's find some results. Let's go get it free to 100 people if we can. And whatever it is, because those results become the sales message. People always ask me, Russell, what's the future of internet marketing? The future of internet marketing is less selling and more like getting results for people than showing those results. That's the easiest sales video ever, is actually help someone get a result and then just document and show that. That's better than any copywriting course you could ever go through if you capture them right. So that's kind of the thing. Now the way these ask campaigns work, and this is even if you have an existing business, still anytime you create a new offer, a new product, or do anything, or you're stagnant, or you can't figure things out, we use these all the time. We're bringing them out every time we're trying to figure out the next piece. So it comes through here, and you ask them what's number one question. And what will happen is you start getting a whole bunch of different things coming in. So when we did this for my potato gun product initially, I started getting all these questions coming in, right? And I ran it until I had about 100 questions.
